{
  "extract_plan": {
    "section_map": [
      {"heading": "Title: AI Governance In Action: Harvey LAB Benchmark Puts Claude, Gemini, and ChatGPT to the Test", "location": "title"},
      {"heading": "Subtitle / dek", "location": "subtitle"},
      {"heading": "Intro (Emergence World / neither scored above 10%)", "location": "intro"},
      {"heading": "What Is The Harvey LAB Benchmark", "location": "what-is-lab"},
      {"heading": "Benchmark Components : What LAB Tests", "location": "benchmark-components"},
      {"heading": "Scoring Methodology", "location": "scoring-methodology"},
      {"heading": "What Triggered The Test", "location": "what-triggered"},
      {"heading": "The Outcomes:", "location": "outcomes"},
      {"heading": "Why Each Model Struggled: Jagged Intelligence", "location": "jagged-intelligence"},
      {"heading": "Cost & Latency: The Hidden Problem", "location": "cost-latency"},
      {"heading": "Behavioral Insights: What Successful Agents Do", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
      {"heading": "Conclusion", "location": "conclusion"}
    ],
    "concept_inventory": [
      "AI governance", "Harvey LAB benchmark", "long-horizon legal work", "legal reasoning/synthesis/judgment",
      "frontier models", "large language models", "auditable evaluation framework", "open source",
      "all-pass rubric scoring", "end-to-end pass rate", "latency", "cost per task", "rubric checks",
      "realistic task set", "execution harness", "courtroom hallucinations", "sanctions",
      "regulatory and procurement pressure", "jagged intelligence", "speed/accuracy/cost trade-off",
      "agent architecture", "workflow design", "post-draft validation", "iterative revision loops",
      "thorough research before drafting", "malpractice", "high-precision legal work",
      "partner-level assignments", "liability/confidentiality/reputational risk", "skilled human associate"
    ]
  },
  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_ai_governance", "name": "AI governance", "aliases": ["AI governance teams"], "description": "Organizational practice of governing AI use, here framed as turning model claims into governance evidence for firms, courts, and regulators.", "grounding_span": "AI Governance In Action", "location": "title"},
    {"id": "c_lab", "name": "Harvey LAB benchmark", "aliases": ["LAB", "Legal Agent Benchmark"], "description": "Open-source auditable evaluation framework built by Harvey to measure how reliably LLMs perform real legal work.", "grounding_span": "auditable evaluation framework(called LAB)", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_long_horizon_legal_work", "name": "long-horizon legal work", "aliases": ["real legal work"], "description": "Realistic, multi-step legal tasks rather than average prompt/response requests.", "grounding_span": "long‑horizon legal work", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_legal_reasoning", "name": "legal reasoning, synthesis, and judgment", "aliases": [], "description": "The judgment-oriented legal capabilities LAB is designed to measure.", "grounding_span": "legal reasoning, synthesis, and judgment oriented tasks", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_frontier_models", "name": "frontier models", "aliases": ["leading frontier models"], "description": "The leading large language models (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) benchmarked on legal tasks.", "grounding_span": "the leading frontier models", "location": "intro"},
    {"id": "c_llm", "name": "large language models", "aliases": ["LLMs"], "description": "The class of models whose reliability on legal work LAB measures.", "grounding_span": "how reliably large language models perform", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_auditable_eval_framework", "name": "auditable evaluation framework", "aliases": [], "description": "An evaluation framework whose tasks, rubrics, harness and artifacts are published for reproduction and audit.", "grounding_span": "auditable evaluation framework(called LAB)", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_open_source", "name": "open source", "aliases": [], "description": "The benchmark's tasks, rubrics and harness code are published openly.", "grounding_span": "open source", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_all_pass_rubric", "name": "all-pass rubric scoring", "aliases": ["All-pass scoring rule", "binary end-to-end standard"], "description": "Scoring where a task passes only if every rubric criterion is satisfied.", "grounding_span": "all‑pass rubric", "location": "scoring-methodology"},
    {"id": "c_end_to_end_pass_rate", "name": "end-to-end pass rate", "aliases": [], "description": "Core metric: fraction of tasks passing the strict all-pass standard.", "grounding_span": "end‑to‑end pass rate", "location": "outcomes"},
    {"id": "c_latency", "name": "latency", "aliases": [], "description": "Core metric: time taken to complete a task.", "grounding_span": "latency", "location": "outcomes"},
    {"id": "c_cost_per_task", "name": "cost per task", "aliases": [], "description": "Core metric: monetary cost to complete a single task.", "grounding_span": "cost per task", "location": "outcomes"},
    {"id": "c_rubric_checks", "name": "rubric checks", "aliases": ["granular rubrics"], "description": "Expert-written, itemized checks (thousands across the task set) used to evaluate tasks.", "grounding_span": "the benchmark uses thousands of rubric checks", "location": "benchmark-components"},
    {"id": "c_realistic_task_set", "name": "realistic task set", "aliases": ["partner-level assignments"], "description": "Realistic, messy partner-level legal assignments each with instruction, client matter and deliverable.", "grounding_span": "Realistic Task Set", "location": "benchmark-components"},
    {"id": "c_execution_harness", "name": "execution harness", "aliases": [], "description": "Reproducible tooling that runs agents against tasks with provider adapters and parallel sweeps.", "grounding_span": "Execution harness", "location": "benchmark-components"},
    {"id": "c_courtroom_hallucination", "name": "courtroom hallucinations", "aliases": ["fake cases", "non-existent cases"], "description": "AI-generated fabricated case citations submitted in legal briefs.", "grounding_span": "Courtroom hallucinations made the risk visible", "location": "what-triggered"},
    {"id": "c_sanctions", "name": "sanctions", "aliases": [], "description": "Penalties imposed on lawyers/judges for submitting AI-generated briefs with fake cases.", "grounding_span": "produced sanctions, headlines", "location": "what-triggered"},
    {"id": "c_regulatory_pressure", "name": "regulatory and procurement pressure for auditable proof", "aliases": [], "description": "Pressure from regulators, bar associations and procurement teams for documented validation of high-risk AI.", "grounding_span": "regulators, bar associations, and corporate procurement teams push for documented validation of high‑risk AI", "location": "what-triggered"},
    {"id": "c_jagged_intelligence", "name": "jagged intelligence", "aliases": [], "description": "Model capability is unevenly distributed across legal domains; no model leads everywhere.", "grounding_span": "jagged intelligence", "location": "jagged-intelligence"},
    {"id": "c_cost_latency_tradeoff", "name": "speed vs. accuracy vs. cost trade-off", "aliases": ["operational trade-off"], "description": "Operational trade-off firms face because the best models are the slowest and most expensive.", "grounding_span": "speed vs. accuracy vs. cost", "location": "cost-latency"},
    {"id": "c_agent_architecture", "name": "agent architecture", "aliases": [], "description": "How an agent is structured, argued to matter as much as the underlying model.", "grounding_span": "agent architecture", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "c_workflow_design", "name": "workflow design", "aliases": [], "description": "Design of the agent's workflow, argued to matter as much as the underlying model.", "grounding_span": "workflow design", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "c_post_draft_validation", "name": "post-draft validation", "aliases": [], "description": "Behavior of high-performing agents: validating output after drafting.", "grounding_span": "post‑draft validation", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "c_iterative_revision", "name": "iterative revision loops", "aliases": [], "description": "Behavior of high-performing agents: revising iteratively.", "grounding_span": "iterative revision loops", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "c_thorough_research", "name": "thorough research before drafting", "aliases": [], "description": "Behavior of high-performing agents: researching thoroughly before drafting.", "grounding_span": "thorough research before drafting", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "c_malpractice", "name": "malpractice", "aliases": [], "description": "In legal practice partial correctness can be counted as malpractice, motivating all-pass scoring.", "grounding_span": "partial correctness can still be counted as malpractice", "location": "scoring-methodology"},
    {"id": "c_high_precision_legal", "name": "high-precision legal work", "aliases": [], "description": "Legal work requiring high precision, for which fast cheap models are nearly useless.", "grounding_span": "high‑precision legal work", "location": "cost-latency"},
    {"id": "c_partner_assignments", "name": "partner-level assignments", "aliases": ["partner-style assignments"], "description": "M&A data-room review, regulatory synthesis, litigation drafting, contract redlines, corporate transaction workflows.", "grounding_span": "Partner‑level assignments", "location": "benchmark-components"},
    {"id": "c_legal_stakes", "name": "liability, client confidentiality, and reputational risk", "aliases": [], "description": "The realistic stakes of legal work that LAB aligns evaluation with.", "grounding_span": "liability, client confidentiality, and reputational risk", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "c_skilled_human_associate", "name": "skilled human associate workflow", "aliases": [], "description": "High-performing agents mirror the workflow of a skilled human associate.", "grounding_span": "skilled human associate", "location": "behavioral-insights"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_lab", "term": "LAB", "verbatim_text": "an open source, auditable evaluation framework(called LAB) that measures how reliably large language models perform", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-06-01", "grounding_span": "an open source, auditable evaluation framework(called LAB) that measures how reliably large language models perform", "location": "what-is-lab"},
    {"id": "d_all_pass", "term": "All-pass scoring rule", "verbatim_text": "A task only “passes” if **every** rubric criterion is satisfied.", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-06-01", "grounding_span": "A task only “passes” if **every** rubric criterion is satisfied.", "location": "benchmark-components"},
    {"id": "d_jagged", "term": "jagged intelligence", "verbatim_text": "model capability is **unevenly distributed** across legal domains", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026-06-01", "grounding_span": "model capability is **unevenly distributed** across legal domains", "location": "jagged-intelligence"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_under10", "claim_text": "Top models passed well under 10 percent of tasks under the strict standard, and the best models were also the slowest and most expensive.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Headline results showed top models passing well under 10 percent of tasks under the strict standard, with the best models also being the slowest and most expensive.", "location": "outcomes"},
    {"id": "cl_neither10", "claim_text": "Neither of the leading frontier models scored above 10%.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "neither scored above 10%.", "location": "intro"},
    {"id": "cl_thousands", "claim_text": "The benchmark uses thousands of rubric checks across the task set.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the benchmark uses thousands of rubric checks", "location": "benchmark-components"},
    {"id": "cl_chatgpt", "claim_text": "ChatGPT-5.5 excels in regulated industries and emerging-company work requiring heavy research.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "excels in **regulated industries** and **emerging‑company work** requiring heavy research.", "location": "jagged-intelligence"},
    {"id": "cl_claude_corp", "claim_text": "Claude Opus 4.7 excels in corporate transactions, where synthesis and structured reasoning matter.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "excels in **corporate transactions** , where synthesis and structured reasoning matter.", "location": "jagged-intelligence"},
    {"id": "cl_nomodel", "claim_text": "No model leads in every practice area; the leaderboard shifts dramatically depending on the domain.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the leaderboard shifts dramatically depending on the domain.", "location": "jagged-intelligence"},
    {"id": "cl_claude_cost", "claim_text": "Claude Opus 4.7 costs ~$50.90 per task and takes ~22 minutes to complete a single LAB task.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "**Claude Opus 4.7** costs **~$50.90 per task** and takes **~22 minutes** to complete a single LAB task.", "location": "cost-latency"},
    {"id": "cl_gemini", "claim_text": "Gemini 3.5 Flash is much faster (under 6 minutes) but nearly useless for high-precision legal work.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "is much faster (under 6 minutes) but nearly useless for high‑precision legal work.", "location": "cost-latency"},
    {"id": "cl_behavior", "claim_text": "High-performing agents conduct thorough research before drafting.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "thorough research before drafting", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "cl_agentarch", "claim_text": "Agent architecture and workflow design matter as much as the underlying model.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "**agent architecture** and **workflow design** matter as much as the underlying model.", "location": "behavioral-insights"},
    {"id": "cl_win", "claim_text": "Smart firms that leverage AI's strengths as a flawed junior associate under strict supervision will win.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "smart firms who leverage its strengths as a powerful, flawed junior associate (with strict supervision) will win.", "location": "conclusion"}
  ],
